title: Corner-Shape, Spiral, Proximity Type, Magnetics, Scroll-State
description: Five bleeding-edge UI patterns: scroll-driven corner morphs, CSS spiral layout, proximity variable font, spring magnetics, and scroll-state() sticky nav.

drops.title: Corner-Shape Scroll Morph
drops.description: CSS corner-shape: superellipse() tied to scroll-driven animation — cards morph from concave scoops to sharp squircles as you scroll.
drops.concept: The new CSS corner-shape property controls corner curvature via superellipse(n) where n ranges from -∞ (extreme scoop) through 0 (bevel), 1 (round), 2 (squircle), to +∞ (sharp). Binding it to animation-timeline: scroll() creates a zero-JS, perfectly smooth morph as the user scrolls.
drops.source: css-tricks.com — Experimenting With Scroll-Driven corner-shape Animations

interaction.scroll: Scroll the page to watch corner shapes morph

drops.title: sibling-index() Spiral
drops.description: CSS sibling-index() arranges each character's rotation and radius in a logarithmic spiral — no JS, no SVG path, pure CSS positioning.
drops.concept: sibling-index() is a new CSS function that returns an integer for each element's position among its siblings. By using it inside calc() to drive rotation angle, translateX radius, and scale, you create a natural spiral. Three variants (forward, reversed, offset) demonstrate how changing the starting angle or direction produces entirely different visual outcomes.
drops.source: css-tricks.com — Spiral Scrollytelling in CSS With sibling-index()

interaction.mouse: Hover a spiral to rotate it by 45°
interaction.click: Click word buttons to change the text

drops.title: Proximity Variable Font
drops.description: Letters morph weight and color in real time based on Euclidean distance from the cursor — seven concentric rings of influence.
drops.concept: Each character is a separate span. On every mousemove event, the JS calculates distance from cursor to each letter's center, then classifies it into one of 7 rings with CSS class swaps. Each ring maps to a font-weight (900 → 200) and a color (teal → dim purple). The result: text appears to have gravitational mass that bends typography around the cursor.
drops.source: Codrops — The Exat Microsite: Pushing a Typography Showcase to New Creative Extremes

interaction.mouse: Move cursor over text to warp its weight
interaction.touch: Touch events not ideal — effect requires pointer

drops.title: Magnetic Spring Buttons
drops.description: Buttons spring toward the cursor with tunable range, strength and damping — a grid of 9 colorful variants plus three style demos.
drops.concept: For each button, the distance from cursor to button center is computed every animation frame. Within the influence radius, a spring force proportional to proximity attracts the button toward the cursor. A velocity + damping system (spring constant + friction) prevents oscillation. The inner text translates opposite at 0.3× for a parallax illusion. A debug panel lets you tune range (30–200px), strength (0–1), and spring stiffness.
drops.source: codrops/MagneticButtons + Medium/@fulton_shaun — Magnetic Buttons with CSS Only

interaction.mouse: Move cursor near any button to attract it
interaction.click: Click range/strength/spring sliders to tune behavior

drops.title: Scroll-State Sticky Nav
drops.description: A navbar that compacts, gains blur and border purely in CSS when stuck — using the new @container scroll-state(stuck: top) query.
drops.concept: scroll-state() is a CSS container query variant that detects scroll-related states: stuck (for sticky elements), snapped (for scroll-snap targets), and scrollable (for overflow containers). Wrapping a sticky nav in container-type: scroll-state lets child CSS rules target it with @container nav-container scroll-state(stuck: top) — no JavaScript, no IntersectionObserver sentinels needed. A JS fallback using IntersectionObserver handles older browsers.
drops.source: css-tricks.com — Scrollytelling on Steroids With Scroll-State Queries

interaction.scroll: Scroll the page down to watch the nav compact; scroll back to expand
